name: find-skills
title: find-skills
description: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill.

name: web-design-guidelines
title: web-design-guidelines
description: Review UI code for Web Interface Guidelines compliance. Use when asked to "review my UI", "check accessibility", "audit design", "review UX", or "check my site against best practices".

name: frontend-design
title: frontend-design
description: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, artifacts, posters, or applications (examples include websites, landing pages, dashboards, React components, HTML/CSS layouts, or when styling/beautifying any web UI). Generates creative, polished code and UI design that avoids generic AI aesthetics.

name: agent-tools
title: agent-tools
description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok

name: remotion-render
title: remotion-render
description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video

name: ai-image-generation
title: ai-image-generation
description: Generate AI images with FLUX, Gemini, Grok, Seedream, Reve and 50+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: FLUX Dev LoRA, FLUX.2 Klein LoRA, Gemini 3 Pro Image, Grok Imagine, Seedream 4.5, Reve, ImagineArt. Capabilities: text-to-image, image-to-image, inpainting, LoRA, image editing, upscaling, text rendering. Use for: AI art, product mockups, concept art, social media graphics, marketing visuals, illustrations. Triggers: flux, image generation, ai image, text to image, stable diffusion, generate image, ai art, midjourney alternative, dall-e alternative, text2img, t2i, image generator, ai picture, create image with ai, generative ai, ai illustration, grok image, gemini image

name: ai-video-generation
title: ai-video-generation
description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative

name: twitter-automation
title: twitter-automation
description: Automate Twitter/X with posting, engagement, and user management via inference.sh CLI. Apps: x/post-tweet, x/post-create (with media), x/post-like, x/post-retweet, x/dm-send, x/user-follow. Capabilities: post tweets, schedule content, like posts, retweet, send DMs, follow users, get profiles. Use for: social media automation, content scheduling, engagement bots, audience growth, X API. Triggers: twitter api, x api, tweet automation, post to twitter, twitter bot, social media automation, x automation, tweet scheduler, twitter integration, post tweet, twitter post, x post, send tweet

name: expo-skills
title: Expo Skills Collection
description: A collection of AI agent skills for working with Expo projects and Expo Application Services

name: docx
title: docx
description: Create, edit, analyze Word docs with tracked changes, comments, formatting.

name: pdf
title: pdf
description: Extract text, tables, metadata, merge & annotate PDFs.

name: pptx
title: pptx
description: Read, generate, and adjust slides, layouts, templates.

name: xlsx
title: xlsx
description: Spreadsheet manipulation: formulas, charts, data transformations.

name: markdown-to-epub-converter
title: Markdown to EPUB Converter
description: Converts markdown documents and chat summaries into professional EPUB ebook files. *By [@smerchek](https://github.com/smerchek)*

name: artifacts-builder
title: artifacts-builder
description: Suite of tools for creating elaborate, multi-component claude.ai HTML artifacts using modern frontend web technologies (React, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui).

name: aws-skills
title: aws-skills
description: AWS development with CDK best practices, cost optimization MCP servers, and serverless/event-driven architecture patterns.

name: claude-code-terminal-title
title: Claude Code Terminal Title
description: Gives each Claud-Code terminal window a dynamic title that describes the work being done so you don't lose track of what window is doing what.

name: ffuf-web-fuzzing
title: FFUF Web Fuzzing
description: Integrates the ffuf web fuzzer so Claude can run fuzzing tasks and analyze results for vulnerabilities. *By [@jthack](https://github.com/jthack)*

name: finishing-a-development-branch
title: finishing-a-development-branch
description: Guides completion of development work by presenting clear options and handling chosen workflow.

name: ios-simulator
title: iOS Simulator
description: Enables Claude to interact with iOS Simulator for testing and debugging iOS applications. *By [@conorluddy](https://github.com/conorluddy)*

name: move-code-quality-skill
title: move-code-quality-skill
description: Analyzes Move language packages against the official Move Book Code Quality Checklist for Move 2024 Edition compliance and best practices.

name: playwright-browser-automation
title: Playwright Browser Automation
description: Model-invoked Playwright automation for testing and validating web applications. *By [@lackeyjb](https://github.com/lackeyjb)*

name: pypict-claude-skill
title: pypict-claude-skill
description: Design comprehensive test cases using PICT (Pairwise Independent Combinatorial Testing) for requirements or code, generating optimized test suites with pairwise coverage.

name: skill-seekers
title: Skill Seekers
description: Automatically converts any documentation website into a Claude AI skill in minutes. *By [@yusufkaraaslan](https://github.com/yusufkaraaslan)*

name: test-driven-development
title: test-driven-development
description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code.

name: using-git-worktrees
title: using-git-worktrees
description: Creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification.

name: webapp-testing
title: Webapp Testing
description: Tests local web applications using Playwright for verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, and capturing screenshots.

name: csv-data-summarizer
title: CSV Data Summarizer
description: Automatically analyzes CSV files and generates comprehensive insights with visualizations without requiring user prompts. *By [@coffeefuelbump](https://github.com/coffeefuelbump)*

name: root-cause-tracing
title: root-cause-tracing
description: Use when errors occur deep in execution and you need to trace back to find the original trigger.

name: article-extractor
title: article-extractor
description: Extract full article text and metadata from web pages.

name: brainstorming
title: brainstorming
description: Transform rough ideas into fully-formed designs through structured questioning and alternative exploration.

name: youtube-transcript
title: youtube-transcript
description: Fetch transcripts from YouTube videos and prepare summaries.

name: ship-learn-next
title: ship-learn-next
description: Skill to help iterate on what to build or learn next, based on feedback loops.

name: tapestry
title: tapestry
description: Interlink and summarize related documents into knowledge networks.

name: git-pushing
title: git-pushing
description: Automate git operations and repository interactions.

name: review-implementing
title: review-implementing
description: Evaluate code implementation plans and align with specs.

name: test-fixing
title: test-fixing
description: Detect failing tests and propose patches or fixes.

name: computer-forensics
title: computer-forensics
description: Digital forensics analysis and investigation techniques.

name: file-deletion
title: file-deletion
description: Secure file deletion and data sanitization methods.

name: metadata-extraction
title: metadata-extraction
description: Extract and analyze file metadata for forensic purposes.

name: mongodb-connection
title: MongoDB Connection Optimizer
description: Optimize MongoDB client connection configuration (pools, timeouts, patterns) for any supported driver language. Use when instantiating or configuring a MongoDB client, troubleshooting connection errors (ECONNREFUSED, timeouts, pool exhaustion), building serverless functions with MongoDB, or optimizing connection-related performance in high-traffic applications.

name: mongodb-mcp-setup
title: MongoDB MCP Server Setup
description: Guides users through configuring the MongoDB MCP server for use with an agentic client. Covers authentication options: direct connection strings, service account credentials (recommended for Atlas users), or local Atlas development via Docker. Use when a user has the MongoDB MCP server installed but hasn't configured required environment variables.

name: mongodb-natural-language-querying
title: MongoDB Natural Language Querying
description: Generates read-only MongoDB queries and aggregation pipelines from natural language descriptions, using collection schema context and sample documents. Use when asked to write, create, or generate MongoDB queries; to filter, query, or aggregate data; or to translate SQL-like requests to MongoDB syntax.

name: mongodb-query-optimizer
title: MongoDB Query Optimizer
description: MongoDB query optimization and indexing assistance. Analyzes slow queries, recommends indexes, and explains performance issues using MongoDB MCP tools and Atlas Performance Advisor. Use when asked to optimize a query, add indexes, diagnose slow queries, or review cluster performance.

name: mongodb-schema-design
title: MongoDB Schema Design
description: Guidelines for MongoDB data modeling patterns and anti-patterns. Covers embedding vs. referencing, schema validation, document sizing, unbounded arrays, one-to-many relationships, TTL, tree structures, and migration from relational databases. Use when designing schemas, reviewing data models, or migrating from SQL.

name: mongodb-search-and-ai
title: MongoDB Search & AI (Atlas Search, Vector, RAG)
description: Guides implementation of Atlas Search (full-text/lexical), Vector Search (semantic similarity), and Hybrid Search. Covers autocomplete, fuzzy matching, faceted search, embeddings, RAG applications, and multi-field text filtering. Use when adding search to a MongoDB collection or building AI-powered retrieval.

name: atlas-stream-processing
title: MongoDB Atlas Stream Processing
description: Manages MongoDB Atlas Stream Processing (ASP) workflows: workspace provisioning, data source/sink connections, processor lifecycle, debugging diagnostics, and tier sizing. Supports Kafka, Atlas clusters, S3, HTTPS, and Lambda integrations. Use for streaming pipelines, event processing, and real-time data workflows.

name: qdrant-clients-sdk
title: Qdrant Client SDK Integration
description: Guides integration with Qdrant vector database using official client SDKs for Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Rust, Go, .NET, and Java. Covers REST and gRPC APIs, code examples, installation, vector storage, and similarity search setup.

name: qdrant-deployment-options
title: Qdrant Deployment Options
description: Guides selection of the appropriate Qdrant deployment: local mode for prototyping, self-hosted Docker for production, managed cloud for zero-ops, or EDGE for ultra-low latency. Use when choosing between deployment types or asking 'how to deploy Qdrant', 'Docker vs Cloud', or 'self-hosted vs cloud'.

name: qdrant-model-migration
title: Qdrant Embedding Model Migration
description: Guides zero-downtime embedding model migration in Qdrant. Use when switching embedding models, migrating vectors, upgrading model dimensions, switching providers, A/B testing models, or asking 'how to re-embed my data' or 'can I use two models at once'.

name: qdrant-monitoring
title: Qdrant Monitoring & Observability
description: Guides Qdrant monitoring and observability setup using Prometheus, Grafana, and health checks. Use when asking how to monitor Qdrant, what metrics to track, why memory is growing, requests are slow, or when debugging production issues requiring metric analysis.

name: qdrant-performance-optimization
title: Qdrant Performance Optimization
description: Techniques to optimize Qdrant performance including indexing strategies, query optimization, and hardware considerations. Covers search latency, throughput, indexing build times, memory usage, and storage balancing between memory and disk.

name: qdrant-scaling
title: Qdrant Scaling Guide
description: Guides Qdrant scaling decisions: vertical vs horizontal, sharding, node sizing, multi-tenant setups, and capacity planning. Use when data doesn't fit on one node, cluster is slow, need more throughput, too many tenants, or asking 'how many nodes do I need'.

name: qdrant-search-quality
title: Qdrant Search Quality
description: Diagnoses and improves Qdrant search relevance. Use when search results are bad, wrong, irrelevant, or missing; when precision or recall is low; when choosing embedding models; or when quality degrades after quantization, model change, or data growth. Also covers hybrid search and reranking decisions.

name: qdrant-version-upgrade
title: Qdrant Version Upgrade
description: Guidance on upgrading Qdrant versions without interrupting application availability and ensuring data integrity. Covers version compatibility, storage compatibility, rolling upgrades for zero-downtime, multi-minor-version migrations, and SDK/server version matching.

name: neo4j-cypher-skill
title: Neo4j Cypher Query Upgrade
description: Upgrades Neo4j 4.x and 5.x Cypher queries to 2025.x/2026.x versions. Use when migrating a database from Neo4j version 4.x or 5.x to a newer major version, or when upgrading legacy Cypher queries for compatibility.

name: neo4j-cli-tools-skill
title: Neo4j CLI Tools
description: Guidance on Neo4j command-line tools: neo4j-admin, cypher-shell, aura-cli, and neo4j-mcp. Covers database administration, Cypher query execution, Aura cloud management, MCP server setup for AI agents, migration, backup, and troubleshooting CLI operations.

name: neo4j-migration-skill
title: Neo4j Driver Migration
description: Upgrades Neo4j drivers to new major versions across .NET, Go, Java, JavaScript, and Python. Use when migrating legacy Neo4j codebases to newer driver versions.
